summary: Fixed generated sanitizeJSONResponse to strip the comma-suffixed XSSI prefix (")]}',") before the shorter no-comma variant, so widget/picker responses parse.
reason: Google's widget/picker endpoints prefix responses with ")]}'," (5 bytes, includes a trailing comma), not just ")]}'" (4 bytes) like /api/explore. The generated sanitizeJSONResponse only checked the 4-byte variants; since ")]}'" is itself a byte-prefix of ")]}',", it matched first and stripped only 4 bytes, leaving a stray leading comma that broke every json.Unmarshal call on widget/picker data ("invalid character ',' looking for beginning of value"). This is a bug in the printing-press generator's own sanitizeJSONResponse template, not specific to this CLI; confirmed live by dumping the raw response body.
validated_outcome: Live: trends interest/region/related/geo-gap all return real parsed data from a clean cookie/cache state after this fix.
